arts.sum: January is not an easy month, not an easy time to bring people in, and at the same time we still want to make sure we do what we think is the best thing for us rather than panic and bring people in who aren’t going to be a suitable fit." "The club is working hard to try to get some help for the playing group and it’s no secret, we need to bolster our numbers up a little bit, so the club's working hard on that.

arts.sum: Asked if Botman would feel like a 'new signing' upon his return, Howe agreed when saying: "It is a cliche but it's a good one. Eddie Howe admits Sven Botman will feel like a new signing as he is welcomed back into the fold for Newcastle United .

arts.ttl: Tottenham vs Newcastle United postponement latest after Met Office issue snow and ice warning
arts.sum: However, while slim, there is a chance the game does not actually go ahead with the Met Office issuing a weather warning for snow for this weekend. Newcastle United will be looking to continue their excellent recent form in their next Premier League clash away at Tottenham.

arts.ttl: Tottenham hit by mass sickness bug outbreak as several stars in Ange Postecoglou's depleted squad miss training just one day ahead of tough test against Newcastle
arts.sum: Tottenham's preparations for the visit of Newcastle have been disrupted by illness within the squad, manager Ange Postecoglou has confirmed.

Tottenham have signed Denmark midfielder Olivia Holdt on a contract running until June 2027.

arts.ttl: Spurs team Postecoglou to pick vs Newcastle with Bergvall chance and Son call
arts.sum: Guest's team: Forster; Porro, Dragusin, Gray, Spence; Bissouma, Sarr, Maddison; Kulusevski, Solanke, Johnson. The Tottenham squad to take on Newcastle United could be quite interesting going on the amount of injuries at present and also the sickness bug Ange Postecoglou alluded to in his press conference.

arts.ttl: Club ‘would not oppose’ January transfer as Leicester and Tottenham eye player – Asking price not high
arts.sum: Udinese would be willing to sell defender Thomas Kristensen this month if Tottenham or Leicester City make moves for him. That’s according to Messaggero Veneto, who say both sides have shown an interest in the 22-year-old in this window. A report in England named Leicester and Tottenham as suitors for the Danish centre-back this week, The post Club ‘would not oppose’ January transfer as Leicester and Tottenham eye player – Asking price not high appeared first on Sport Witness.
